Title: Bisphenol A causes melatonin biosynthesis epigenetic reprogramming of melatonin biosynthesis genes in Arabidopsis thaliana

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as one of five BPA-responsive hub genes from RNA-seq/GNN network analysis. 2. Expression was downregulated by BPA in Arabidopsis protoplasts. 3. Its promoter contains an M1-M2 assembly and shows BPA-induced reduction of H3K9ac, supporting epigenetic repression associated with BPA response.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BPA represses AT1G01300 expression via promoter-associated histone deacetylation, linking the gene to oxidative/redox response under BPA stress.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; protoplast BPA-sensitive reporter assay; qRT-PCR/expression analysis; ChIP-qPCR for H3K9ac
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated after BPA exposure in protoplasts
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter fragments in BPA-sensitive vectors; CaMV 35S used in reporter constructs
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GSA: CRA026859; manuscript also reports GSA: subCRA025822 for RNA-seq submission
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: BPA exposure; melatonin co-treatment in some assays
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could contribute to BPA-responsive biomonitoring module when combined with M1-M2 promoter assembly, but direct metabolic engineering value is not established.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as a negative regulator of cGMP-dependent methionine oxidation in the Discussion; not demonstrated as direct melatonin biosynthetic enzyme.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as one of five BPA-responsive hub genes. 2. Expression was downregulated by BPA. 3. Promoter contains only M1 motifs and does not show BPA-induced H3K9ac/H3K14ac changes, indicating M2 is required for the histone acetylation-based response. 4. Used in reporter systems to assess BPA sensitivity.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BPA represses AT1G19670 expression through an M1-associated response, but without the M2-dependent histone acetylation repression observed for AT1G26220/AT1G01300.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; protoplast reporter assay; qRT-PCR/expression analysis; ChIP-qPCR
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated after BPA exposure in protoplasts
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter fragments in BPA-sensitive vectors; CaMV 35S used in reporter constructs
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GSA: CRA026859; manuscript also reports GSA: subCRA025822
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a BPA-responsive reporter component, though less sensitive than AT1G26220-based system.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion describes it as a chloroplast biogenesis gene with consecutive M1 motifs; not a direct melatonin pathway gene.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as one of five BPA-responsive hub genes. 2. Expression was upregulated by BPA and centrally associated with AT1G26220 in the simplified response network. 3. Used in protoplast reporter assays, showing loss of red fluorescence at high BPA concentrations.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a BPA-responsive hub gene upregulated during BPA stress, but no direct causal role in melatonin biosynthesis was demonstrated.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; GNN/network analysis; protoplast reporter assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated after BPA exposure in protoplasts
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter fragments in BPA-sensitive vectors
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GSA: CRA026859; manuscript also reports GSA: subCRA025822
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential BPA biomonitoring marker, but no direct nutrient-metabolism engineering role shown.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as associated with AT1G26220 in network center; mechanistic role in metabolite control remains unclear.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as one of five BPA-responsive hub genes. 2. Expression was upregulated by BPA. 3. Included in reporter systems and showed strong response to BPA in fluorescence assays.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BPA-responsive hub gene used for BPA detection; direct role in melatonin pathway was not established.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; GNN/network analysis; protoplast reporter assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated after BPA exposure in protoplasts
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter fragments in BPA-sensitive vectors
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GSA: CRA026859; manuscript also reports GSA: subCRA025822
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The text contains a likely typo referring to AT3G19540 in one sentence; hub gene list and table support AT3G19450.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream/committed acetylation step in melatonin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetylates serotonin to form N-acetylserotonin (NAS), a direct melatonin precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Reported as a rate-limiting enzyme in melatonin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BPA specifically represses SNAT2 expression through promoter M1-M2-mediated reduction of H3K9ac and H3K14ac, shifting melatonin biosynthesis from a SNAT2-dependent pathway to an alternative SNAT1-dependent pathway while endogenous melatonin rises under stress.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AT1G26220/SNAT2 was identified as the most BPA-sensitive hub gene. 2. BPA binds promoter M1 motifs within consecutive M1-M2 assemblies, reducing H3K9ac and H3K14ac and strongly suppressing SNAT2 transcription. 3. Repression of SNAT2 is associated with reprogramming of melatonin biosynthesis toward a more efficient alternative pathway under BPA stress. 4. The M1-M2-AT1G26220 module functions as a highly specific BPA/analogue biomonitoring system.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; protoplast BPA reporter assays; qRT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR for H3K9ac/H3K14ac; motif analysis; molecular docking; molecular dynamics simulation; BPA-resin binding assay; EMSA; metabolite assay for melatonin
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in BPA-treated protoplasts and leaves; especially sensitive in protoplasts
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintains cellular redox homeostasis, scavenges ROS, mitigates BPA-induced oxidative damage
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native SNAT2 promoter/M1-M2 promoter assemblies in reporter constructs; CaMV 35S promoter in optimized sensor vectors
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GSA: CRA026859; manuscript also reports GSA: subCRA025822
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: BPA exposure; exogenous melatonin co-treatment; oxidative stress context
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppression of SNAT2 may reduce the NAS pool from the constitutive pathway; the paper does not report growth/yield penalties of manipulating SNAT2 directly.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for engineering BPA biosensors and for tuning stress-adaptive melatonin pathway switching; promoter M1-M2 arrangement is critical and should be preserved in synthetic constructs.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes transcriptional repression of SNAT2 despite an overall increase in endogenous melatonin, due to compensatory pathway switching rather than SNAT2 activation.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Alternative SNAT1-dependent melatonin biosynthetic route under stress
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream/committed acetylation step in melatonin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetylates serotonin to form N-acetylserotonin in an alternative melatonin biosynthetic route.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key compensatory enzyme under BPA stress; superior catalytic efficiency discussed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SNAT1 expression tends to increase with BPA exposure, compensating for SNAT2 repression and supporting continued or increased melatonin accumulation under BPA stress.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SNAT1 is presented as another key enzyme in melatonin biosynthesis. 2. Its expression generally increased with rising BPA concentrations. 3. The study infers that melatonin biosynthesis shifts from a SNAT2-dependent pathway to a SNAT1-dependent pathway under BPA stress.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR/expression analysis; endogenous melatonin quantification under BPA and melatonin treatments
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Generally upregulated with increasing BPA concentrations
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintains antioxidant capacity and redox homeostasis under BPA stress
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering stress-resilient melatonin biosynthesis when SNAT2-dependent route is repressed.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific accession was not provided in the paper excerpt; its role is supportive/compensatory rather than the primary mechanistic focus.
